{{ gh-test }} {{ rtd }} {{ pypi }}

# pytest-dependency – Manage dependencies of tests

This module is a plugin for the popular Python testing framework
[pytest]. It manages dependencies of tests: you may mark some tests
as dependent from other tests. These tests will then be skipped if
any of the dependencies did fail or has been skipped.

## Documentation

See the [online documentation](target-1).

The example test modules used in the documentation can be found in
doc/examples in the source distribution.

modules.name: pytest_dependency
documentation:
pytest-dependency - Manage dependencies of tests

This pytest plugin manages dependencies of tests.  It allows to mark
some tests as dependent from other tests.  These tests will then be
skipped if any of the dependencies did fail or has been skipped.

functions.name: pytest_dependency.depends
documentation:
Add dependency on other test.

Call pytest.skip() unless a successful outcome of all of the tests in
other has been registered previously. This has the same effect as
the `depends` keyword argument to the {func}`pytest.mark.dependency`
marker. In contrast to the marker, this function may be called at
runtime during a test.

```
param request

the value of the  pytest fixture related
to the current test.

param other

dependencies, a list of names of tests that this
test depends on.  The names of the dependencies must be
adapted to the scope.

type other

iterable of 

param scope

the scope to search for the dependencies.  Must be
either , , , or .

type scope

```

:::{versionadded} 0.2
:::

:::{versionchanged} 0.5.0 the scope parameter has been added.
:::

functions.name: pytest_dependency.pytest_runtest_makereport
documentation: Store the test outcome if this item is marked "dependency".

functions.name: pytest_dependency.pytest_runtest_setup
documentation:
Check dependencies if this item is marked "dependency".
Skip if any of the dependencies has not been run successfully.

classes.name: pytest_dependency.DependencyItemStatus
documentation: Status of a test item in a dependency manager.

classes.name: pytest_dependency.DependencyManager

methods.name: pytest_dependency.DependencyManager.getManager
documentation:
Get the DependencyManager object from the node at scope level.
Create it, if not yet present.
Dependency manager, stores the results of tests.
